title: pouteria alnifolia var. sacleuxii
intro: Pouteria alnifolia var. sacleuxii, also known as the Tahina palm or palmier poulaga, is a plant species native to Madagascar that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pouteria alnifolia var. sacleuxii include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its use in wound healing and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
